what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment ID to a target image URI and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
standouts: The target URI uses a custom image:// scheme with a channel path and a parenthetical number
Attachment is typed as 'images' rather than a generic file attachment
concerns: The parenthetical '(38)' in the target URI is unexplained and may be an auto-generated filename artifact
Filename includes a bare ID and gives no hint about the image content
notes: This is a lightweight ledger entry that maps an attachment ID to a design-assets image URI and an addition timestamp.
questions: What does the '(38)' in the target URI signify?
Is the target URI resolvable by the system or purely symbolic?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment ledger entry, which matches its stated metadata fields.
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped reference between a channel attachment ID and its underlying image asset.
contribution: Provides a concrete attachment record that links an identifier to an image asset location and creation time, which a fused system could retain for provenance and lookup.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry mapping attachment ID att-16bad0ec in the design-assets channel to the image URI image://design-assets/icons/image (38) with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record when and where a specific image attachment was added to the design-assets channel.
